title: The Keeper's World

|timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0–0.5 ABY]]<ref name="Pizzazz">"[[The Keeper's World]]" and "[[The Kingdom of Ice]]" depict the Rebel Alliance still occupying their base on Yavin 4 after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], meaning that the stories must fall between {{Film|IV}} and the [[Evacuation of Yavin]] as shown in "[[Doom Mission]]". "'''''The Keeper's World'''''" is a serialized comic story published through nine issues of Marvel Comics' magazine ''[[Pizzazz]]'' from October 1977 to June 1978.

The story was reprinted in the UK in [[Star Wars Weekly 47|''Star Wars Weekly'' 47]]–[[Star Wars Weekly 50|50]] in 1978–79, and in the 1999 limited-release comic [[Star Wars 0|''Star Wars'' 0]].

==Plot summary==
Sometime after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] travel toward the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] base in the [[Akuria system/Legends|Akuria system]] to insure against a sudden [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] counterattack on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. En route to the planet, a mistake by [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], who appears to not have been properly repaired after the Battle of Yavin, causes the ship to fly wildly off course. After a near-miss with an Imperial fleet, Luke and Leia encounter four [[Android/Legends|android]] children capable of controlling the elements and a computerized "[[The Keeper|Keeper]]" on a formerly war-torn world who enables them to escape the Imperials.
